raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased an XXXX XXXX for my wife online at Best Buy on XX/XX/XXXX. I went to pick up the XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX at my local store. As part of the transaction, I traded in my wife 's old XXXX for {$230.00}. I delivered it to the store and it was accepted by the Best Buy staff after following specific instructions provided by Best Buy prior to exchanging the phone. 

The Purchase amount ( without the credit ) was {$1100.00}. The Purchase was made with my Best Buy Credit Card provided by Citi. I received my recent statement from Citi on XX/XX/XXXX charging me the full amount of {$1100.00} without the {$230.00} credit. 

I attempted to contact Best Buy Customer service and they advised that it will take up to 30 days to process my credit for redeeming my phone. I stated to the agent that my receipt very clearly states. " Once your trade-in is complete, it may take up to 7 days for your refund to be reflected on your payment method. '' When I quoted this back to the agent, he immediately hung up the phone. 

I want my {$230.00} credit applied to my Citi card. If it isn't credited promptly, I can only conclude that Citi is acting as a facilitator to Unfair, Deceptive, and Abusive Acts and practices. I followed direction provided by Best Buy and provided them with my item. I have receipt of this. Citi can't try to get out of this by putting the blame on Best Buy. If they attempt to, they open themselves up to larger problems.
